Correct L'Ecuyer-CMRG TestU01 failure count

Attached a small documentation patch for the L'Ecuyer-CMRG TestU01 sentence.

I left the Marsaglia newsgroup text unchanged. In the <http://archive.org|archive.org> Usenet
archive, the original 1997-09-29 post with Message-ID
<34303CC2.9101DD78@stat.fsu.edu> is listed under sci.stat.math;
sci.math.num-analysis only has later replies.

Checked Random.Rd with tools::parse_Rd() under R 4.6.0.

*[Bug 19047] Loading a malicious .rds file can crash R*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19047>

Yaoxiang Li (<mailto:liyaoxiang@outlook.com|liyaoxiang@outlook.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |liyaoxiang@outlook.com

--- Comment #3 from Yaoxiang Li (<mailto:liyaoxiang@outlook.com|liyaoxiang@outlook.com>) ---
Created attachment 3705
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3705&action=edit>
Additional unserialize crash reproducer not fixed by attachment 3669

I attached another small unserialize() crash reproducer. It is separate from
the examples in the original report.

On Windows, R 4.6.0 exits with -1073741819. I also applied attachment 3669 to a
fresh SVN checkout at r90065, built rbuild and enough base packages to run
Rscript, and this reproducer still exits with -1073741819.

So attachment 3669 helps with the reported cases, but it does not cover this
input.

text:
*[Bug 19047] Loading a malicious .rds file can crash R*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19047>

--- Comment #4 from Yaoxiang Li (<mailto:liyaoxiang@outlook.com|liyaoxiang@outlook.com>) ---
Created attachment 3706
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3706&action=edit>
Validate ALTREP serialized class metadata

Following up on attachment 3705, I looked further at that reproducer.

On Ubuntu 20.04.4 x86_64, current R-devel r90066 crashes on that input:

```
Rscript (R) version 4.7.0 Under development (unstable) (2026-05-22 r90066)
...
*** caught segfault ***
address 0xe, cause 'memory not mapped'
Traceback:
 1: unserialize(r)
```

The exit status is 139. After applying attachment 3669 and rebuilding, the same
input still crashes in the same way.

The remaining crash is in a different path from the VECSXP recursion case.
A gdb backtrace from r90066 plus attachment 3669 shows:

```
SET_STRING_ELT(..., v=0xe) at memory.c:4296
#1 Rf_ScalarString (x=0xe)
#2 ALTREP_UNSERIALIZE_CLASS at altrep.c:286
#3 ALTREP_UNSERIALIZE_EX at altrep.c:306
#4 ReadItem_Recursive at serialize.c:1922
#8 R_Unserialize at serialize.c:2301
```

I attached a focused patch against `src/main/altrep.c` which validates the
serialized ALTREP class record before using its class symbol, package symbol,
and type fields. With that patch alone on clean r90066, the reproducer gives:

```
raw length: 738
Error in unserialize(r) : invalid ALTREP serialized class
Execution halted
```

I also checked that a small serialize/unserialize smoke test for ordinary
objects still passes, and `tests/reg-tests-1b.R` exits with status 0.
